5 Core Subsequences of Every Launch (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 2
Ry Schwartz: Hey guys. Welcome back to lesson four of the first module. This is just going to
be a very quick overarching view of those five core subsequences I alluded to
earlier and these are the ones that are really going to go ... That we're really
going to go deeper into through modules three through seven. For now, I just
want to give you a big overarching glance at it and then we're going to dive in
deeper throughout the rest of the course.
I also want to remind you at this point that while launch strategies and product
funnels and systems are always going to be evolving and changing, what you're
going to be learning here is designed to complement and play nicely with
whichever method you're using. Whether that's going to be a webinar, live, or
evergreen, whether it's going to be a three-part prerecorded video series, or
workshops, or a seven-day challenge, whatever you're doing, whatever style of
launch you are going to be executing for your funnel, this system is really meant
to just show you those micro-conversions that need to take place across the
journey and the types of emails and sales messaging we use to create and
facilitate that narrative.
With all that said, here is your first look at the full Coaching the Conversion
framework. As you can see, it's really all about moving your prospect from Point
A to Point Buyer, as well as those major milestones that we have to hit along the
way to really get them there, and we're going to look at those in greater detail
in the next lesson as well. In addition to the milestones, we also have those five
major subsequences that are designed to accomplish it. We have the
segmentation, the confirmation, the prelaunch, the launch, the sales, and the
closing. I know that sounds like there's six, but I actually like to tie the
segmentation and confirmation together, because they kind of work together as
a whole. Those are going to be the basis for the rest of the course.
In module three, we're going to dive deeper into the segmentation. We're going
to take a look in confirmation as well, which is probably the most under-
leveraged and under-utilized email in your entire campaign because if you do
that one right, that's where you're setting up that consistency loop, and we're
going to talk about consistency loops as well.
The prelaunch is where you're going to have that opportunity to really re-shift
and reframe those beliefs in that moment of least friction. Your product hasn't
been offered yet, so it's a lot easier and smoother to get your prospect to buy in
to new, more empowering ideas that later on your product is going to support.
The launch simply refers to the moment your course of program first starts
accepting enrollments. There's a huge mistake that so many program creators,
course creators, make at this moment that just scare off potential customers.
We're going to address that full on and show you what this sequence really is
coaching once we get into module five.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

5 Core Subsequences of Every Launch (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 2
In module six, we're going to be talking about the sales emails. You probably
notice right off the bat, there's a distinction we're making between the sales
emails and the closing emails. Most people just loop those in together as
general followups, but you're going to learn why we separate them, and some
of the things that you could get away with in the closing that you definitely want
to avoid doing in the sales. Some things that are actually pretty
counterproductive actually have the reverse opposite effect and it all has to do
with where you place those messages and what you're coaching at the right
time.
We're going to look at those and then, of course, we're going to cap it off with
the closing sequence. This is going to be the last 48 hours of your launch where
you'll typically really ramp up your efforts. You know, be in their inbox maybe
two to four times a day even, and really just hitting them with the messages
that really not just convey urgency, that's just one step. You're going to see
what's actually at play here, what we're actually coaching. Urgency is just one
small part of it. Unless you're really tackling all those other elements that your
prospect needs to see at this point, at this point right before they become a
buyer, then chances are we've been leaving some sales on the table.
Those are the five subsequences at a glance. For now, I just want to leave you
with the takeaway that each of those subsequences has its own role in moving
the prospect across that buyer's journey. Each one is really selling or coaching
its own set of precursors to accomplish its underlying milestone. As we go
deeper into the course, you'll also get a series of email theme plates and guiding
questions to show you exactly how to activate and achieve those micro-
conversions.
That's all you need to get started. I really want to keep you laser focused on
building out these core subsequences before getting too fancy with other types
of subsequences that could be initiated through behavior triggers or
automation. We could sometimes really fall down that rabbit hole. There's a
time and place to do it, and it is really exciting with the tools we have available
to us, but I really want to encourage you to just dial in these five subsequences.
That's what this course is going to be dedicated to. Of course, once that's up
and running, you will get the bonus on [contextology 00:06:02] that will show
you some of the other powerful little profit mines you can create within your
funnel.
That's it for now. Thank you for being along for the ride. In the next module, we
are going to dive into direct response 2.0, which is going to be all about how we
extract the content that goes into this funnel. Take a little breather and I'll catch
you in the next lesson.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
